entries.text: OLE initialization failed.  Make sure that the OLE libraries are the correct version.

entries.name: IDP_FAILED_TO_CREATE
entries.text: Failed to create object.  Make sure the object is entered in the system registry.

entries.name: IDS_JOYSTICK1
entries.text: Center the joystick and press the fire button

entries.name: IDS_JOYSTICK2
entries.text: Move the joystick through its full movement

entries.name: IDS_JOYSTICK3
entries.text: Please check that you are getting full control movement

entries.name: IDS_JOYSTICK5
entries.text: and then press the fire button

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_1_DESC
entries.text:
Approaching Red Interceptors interrupt a UN fighter-bomber strike against a key communist target. The UN Force consists of one squadron of strike aircraft, one squadron tasked with flak suppression and another on fighter escort duty. Two enemy squadrons face them.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_2_DESC
entries.text:
A squadron of UN medium bombers rumbles across the skies of North Korea. Ahead, fighter-bombers drop down to suppress flak batteries around the mission target. Above, fighter escort aircraft react to the sudden appearance of communist interceptor aircraft.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_3_DESC
entries.text:
A UN fighter squadron performing a sweep in MiG Alley discovers an incursion by a squadron of enemy fighters. Neither side has the distinct advantage so both jostle for position.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_4_DESC
entries.text:
While patrolling in MiG Alley, a UN fighter squadron encounters a squadron of enemy interceptors. Each time this scenario is played out, the positional advantage will be set at random.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_5_DESC
entries.text:
A UN fighter patrol finds its catch of the day: a squadron of enemy fighter aircraft has crossed into MiG Alley at a lower altitude where UN jets perform best. The UN patrol is placed above and to the rear of its foe.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_6_DESC
entries.text:
Finding few Red aircraft willing to drop down to the lower altitudes that UN aircraft favor, a squadron of UN fighters forces a conflict by patrolling at high altitude. They are bounced by a Communist squadron that comes screaming in from behind and above.

 Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_7_DESC
entries.text:
A UN armed-reconn mission searches the area along the main highway for enemy supply trucks. Enemy fighters may be patrolling in the local area.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_8_DESC
entries.text:
A UN armed-reconn mission risks heavy ground fire by searching the main rail-line for enemy locomotives. Enemy interceptors may patrol this area.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_9_DESC
entries.text:
Communist Forces launch an air-strike against a key UN facility. A UN patrol nearby reacts to the attack.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_10_DESC
entries.text:
Admire the Korean landscape without enemy interference.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_11_DESC
entries.text:
A UN fighter-bomber squadron expects heavy flak as it performs a strike against a key target south of 'MiG Alley'.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_MARKS_LISTS
entries.text: Mark inserts combo box option ****LISTS**** herte

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_12_DESC
entries.text: You are entering the landing pattern at a new airfield. The Tower has instructed you to land, then taxi around and take off. Repeat this process as many times as you like. The runway has been cleared for your use.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_13_DESC
entries.text:
Communist Forces occupying Seoul have established a bridge-head south of the Han River. Additional Red Forces are crossing by boat to open up the front. We have no forward air control in this area but you are cleared to engage any targets found along the banks of the Han.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_14_DESC
entries.text:
During combat over the Yalu, a UN pilot becomes separated from his flight. Before he can exit the arena he happens across a lone Red fighter in a similar situation. Tactical advantage is randomly determined each time this scenario is played out.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_15_DESC
entries.text:
A UN pilot finds himself in an ideal firing position behind a red fighter. The Chinese pilot is slow to react to the threat.

Click the UN or Red button to view details of each squadron. Click on a pilot icon to select the duty you wish to perform during the mission.

entries.name: IDS_QUICK_16_DESC
entries.text:
A UN armored patrol is approaching the town of Hoengsong via the southern road. Enemy troops have occupied the ridge that overlooks the town and are laying mortar fire onto the convoy. Try to conserve your rockets. T-34 Tanks may exit the town to directly engage the column. You will make your approach from the north. No FAC is available.

Click the UN button to view details of the aircraft performing the mission duty.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OTHERDAMAGE2HI1
entries.text: come on, just a few more should do it...

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OTHERDAMAGE2HI3
entries.text: a few more seconds should do it...

entries.text: are you O.K.? Did you get hurt?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2COWARD
entries.text: I've taken some big hits, I'm bugging out.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2CONV0RR0
entries.text: Ok, I'm going to have to punch out!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_EJECTREPLYSTUCK
entries.text: She won't blow. I'll have to fly her down.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_LOSEPOWERPLUS
entries.text: I'm losing power, I must have taken one in the engine.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_LOSEPOWERSUPERPLUS
entries.text: I'm losing power, I must have taken one up the Scooby!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SMELLSMOKEPLUS
entries.text: I can smell smoke, I must have engine damage.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HAVELEAK
entries.text: yeh, I have a fuel leak, I'm climbing for home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FUELLEAKMSG0
entries.text: my fuel gauge is going down quickly.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FUELLEAKMSG1
entries.text: I think I've got a fuel leak.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_INTROUBLE_REPLY
entries.text: Roger, I know it only too damned well!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_JETTISONREQUEST
entries.text: can we get rid of these tanks?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TOOFAST
entries.text: I can't keep up with you.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CLEARINGAREA0
entries.text: all stores gone, we are turning for home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CLEARINGAREA1
entries.text: we've made our run and are clearing the area.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BRINGAROUND
entries.text: Let's bring it around and make another pass.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SEETARGET
entries.text: I think I can see a target at

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PATROLAREALONG
entries.text: give me a vector to my patrol area.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_NOTABLERESPOND
entries.text: Dentist, we are not able to respond.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HAVETARGETS
entries.text: I have a target for you

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GLADHERE
entries.text: glad you boys are here. We've a situation developing

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CAUTION
entries.text: Caution. Close combat in some positions.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FRIENDRETREATING
entries.text: our guys are RETREATING through the roadblock.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MARKTARGET0
entries.text: Keep me in sight while I mark the target.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MARKTARGET2
entries.text: Watch where I put these rockets.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SEESMOKE1
entries.text: You should see my smoke NOW.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MISSTIMEOUT0
entries.text: No, that was off. Try again.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FAC_SAYS_HIT0
entries.text: A good hit! Pour it on.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FAC_CEASEFIRE0
entries.text: Cease fire. You're hitting friendly forces.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FACTOOHOT1
entries.text: I'll have to leave you to it.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FACNOFRIENDS
entries.text: Just pound the area. It's clear of friendlies.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CUTTOPIECES
entries.text: They are being cut to pieces

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SMOKEOFF0
entries.text: That didn't land where I wanted. Aim

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SMOKEOFF2
entries.text: Smoke landed short of the target.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BANDITSOUTTHERE
entries.text: I've got some bandits out there

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_COMINGINBEHIND
entries.text: we have some MiGs coming in behind us

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_COMINGINABEAM
entries.text: we have some MiGs coming in from

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_COMINGINAHEAD
entries.text: we have some MiGs coming at us

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TAXITOHOLD
entries.text: turn off the runway and taxi back to the holding area.

entries.text: Big train moving out of the station.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MIGSLAUNCHED2
entries.text: dust on the runway at Antung, boys. Heads up.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MIGSLAUNCHED3
entries.text: MiGs are climbing to altitude north of the river.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MIGSLAUNCHED4
entries.text: Bandit train leaving Antung heading south.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MIGSLAUNCHED7
entries.text: I have bandits taking off from

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MULTIPLETRACE
entries.text: Multiple traces, MiGs everywhere today, try

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ALLHEALTHY2
entries.text: No need for anybody to R.T.B.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_AMICLEARTOLAND
entries.text: Tower, am I clear to land?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ALTWINDREQUEST
entries.text: Tower, what's the wind at altitude?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANK5
entries.text: I can't shake off my tanks.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OUTNUMBEREDREPLY0
entries.text: Don't let them get away I'm coming to help.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PILEDIN1
entries.text: Just seen a flash on the ground

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BOASTKILLREPLY3
entries.text: Yeh, Yeh, you got your MiG.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HITBUTOK
entries.text: He was hit, but I think he got out all right.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_INTROUBLE
entries.text: There's a Sabre in trouble down there!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FEELING0
entries.text: This is the day. I got a feeling.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FEELING1
entries.text: I've got a feeling about today.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANK3
entries.text: I've got a problem, one of my tanks has not jettisoned.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANK1
entries.text: I've got a problem, one of my tanks has not jettisoned.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANK2
entries.text: One of my tanks is stuck.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANK6
entries.text: I can't drop a tank, the thing is stuck.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2CONV0RR1
entries.text: Lay your canopy off and eject.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_USEORLOSE
entries.text: Climb for home, use that fuel or lose it!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_NOBODYAVAIL
entries.text: Nobody available to take the lead.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OTHERDAMAGE1LOW2
entries.text: the bullets are ripping into his fuselage!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OTHERDAMAGE1HI0
entries.text: Ok, I'm getting hits on him!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE1COWARD0
entries.text: I've taken some hits, I'm bugging out.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE1COWARD1
entries.text: I've taken some hits, I'm going home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE1LOW2
entries.text: He got me, he got me!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2LOW3
entries.text: I'm hit, get him off me!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2MED3
entries.text: I can't shake him off me!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_PLAYERDAMAGE2HI0
entries.text: I have a real problem here.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BOASTKILL0
entries.text: did you see me get him? I got him!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BOASTKILL1
entries.text: I got one! I got one! Do you see him? Do you see him?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_BOASTKILL3
entries.text: I got one! Wow, he blew up!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OTHERKILLED1
entries.text: Yeh, I hurt him that time.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ENEMYSMOKING1
entries.text: I got him, see that smoke?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ENEMYSMOKING3
entries.text: I hit him, look at that smoke!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FIREINENGINE
entries.text: There's a fire in your engine!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GOTHIMLEAKING0
entries.text: I hit him, he's streaming fuel.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GOTHIMLEAKING1
entries.text: I hit him, he's losing fuel.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ASKFORHELP0
entries.text: I'm under attack, get me some fighters and get them fast!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CLOSEANDFIRING1
entries.text: I've a MiG close in and he's firing!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TURNINSIDE
entries.text: I can't shake him, he's turning inside me!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_CANTHOLD
entries.text: I can't hold him much longer Lead! Get out of there!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OUTNUMBERED0
entries.text: There are MiGs all around me.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_OUTNUMBERED2
entries.text: We're in a turkey shoot here.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TAKEFLIGHTTOMIGS
entries.text: take your flight and sort out those migs!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TAKEFLIGHTTOBANDITS
entries.text: take your flight and sort out those bandits!

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_TOUCHCLOSER
entries.text: We'll see if we can get a touch closer.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_LETGO1
entries.text: We have other things on our plate.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ASSISTCONFIRM
entries.text: Ok, let's go and help them out.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_DEFENDYOURSELVES
entries.text: they are in close, defend yourselves.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ONESWEEP
entries.text: we'll make one more sweep and then go home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GETBACK
entries.text: what are you doing out there? Get back.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GRPGOHOME1
entries.text: Close it up and let's go home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_STARTINGTRGTRUN0
entries.text: ready your bombs and follow me in.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_STARTINGTRGTRUN1
entries.text: ready your napalm and follow me in.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_STARTINGTRGTRUN2
entries.text: ready your rockets and follow me in.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_READYFORTAKEOFF
entries.text: we are ready for takeoff, clear the runway.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANKREPLY0
entries.text: Ok, we won't risk it, R.T.B.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANKREPLY1
entries.text: Ok, Roger that. You'd better go back.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_HUNGTANKREPLY3
entries.text: Ok, Roger, we won't risk it, go home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_EXITANDBUYDRINKS
entries.text: Let's get the hell out of here. I'll buy the drinks.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_ESCORT0
entries.text: escort your buddy out of the combat area.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_GOHOME1
entries.text: go home, get out of the area.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_URGHOME0
entries.text: Get out of there. Head for home.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_SEESMOKEREPLY0
entries.text: I have the target in sight, over.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_FACWORK0
entries.text: Do you have a target, over?

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_WELCOMELONGRANGE
entries.text: In your area but I can't see you.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_LOSTSIGHTLONG
entries.text: I have lost sight of you.

entries.name: IDS_PHRASE_MORETARGETSLONG
entries.text: What else have you got for us?

entries.name: IDS_DISSTART
entries.text: Dis is the start of something... good!

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest01_M
entries.text: UN positions North of Chonan are coming under attack from Chinese troops. They must not be pushed back from this line.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest02_M
entries.text: Forces have engaged on a line between Suwon and Osan.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest03_M
entries.text: The enemy have established a bridge-head and are currently engaging positions south of the Han. More troops are crossing the river by boat.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest04_M
entries.text: The Taeryong River is a natural boundary that the Reds must cross if they are to take their objective, Munsan-ni.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest01_M
entries.text: A major UN offensive has been launched on the IX Corps front. The local division is reporting resistance as it pushes towards the village of Pyongtaek.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest02_M
entries.text: A major UN offensive has been launched on the IX Corps front. Our troops are attempting to move up the main highway to capture Suwon.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest03_M
entries.text: We have launched a new ground offensive in the west sector. Our forces are attempting to dislodge enemy defenders south of the Han River so that a push into Seoul can be made.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest04_M
entries.text: An IX Corps offensive is being held up by Chinese resistance in an area north-west of Munsan-ni.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest05_M
entries.text: IX Corps troops are fighting northwards with the aim of capturing Pyongsan.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackWest06_M
entries.text: IX Corps forces south of Pyongyang are expecting fierce resistance today as they launch an all out assault on the North's Capital.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr01_M
entries.text: Major UN operations are being conducted in the area south of Wonju. X Corps commanders are confident that this key supply point can be taken.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr02_M
entries.text: Our troops have struck out from Wonju to meet the main line of resistance just south of Hoengsong.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr03_M
entries.text: UN forces in this area are eager to break through the Chinese lines and take the key town of Chuncheon.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr04_M
entries.text: X Corps forces are fighting across difficult terrain in this region. The main hotspots are between Chuncheon and the Hwachon Reservoir.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr05_M
entries.text: X Corps has launched an offensive in an area known as the Iron Triangle; a hilly region bounded by Pyonggang, Kumwha and Chorwon.

entries.name: IDS_T_AttackCntr06_M
entries.text: X Corps is engaged in what it hopes will be a final and decisive operation to outflank forces defending the Northern capital.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr01_M
entries.text: The reds have attacked our last line of defense south of Wonju. We must hold them back or the UN will be pushed out of Korea.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr02_M
entries.text: The U.S. 23rd Infantry Regiment and a French battalion are holding the mountain ringed village of Chipyong-ni, some 10 miles Northwest of Wonju. They are being surrounded by enemy forces.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr03_M
entries.text: A major Red offensive has been launched on the line running south of Chuncheon.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr04_M
entries.text: Reds are attempting a new push south from their line at Hwachon.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr05_M
entries.text: The Chinese have amassed enough supplies to launch and sustain a major new offensive. The battle is taking place in an area called the Iron Triangle.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendCntr06_M
entries.text: The Reds have snatched potential victory away from us by mounting a push southwards, away from their last line of defense.

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance01_H
entries.text: UN move north to new line

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance01_M
entries.text: Following their defeat in this sector, Red Troops across the country have withdrawn northwards. The bomb-line now runs from Suwon to Hoengsong. All targets north of this line are Chinese held.

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance02_M
entries.text: The Communists have withdrawn to a new line that passes through Seoul, Chuncheon and Yangyang.

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance03_M
entries.text: Our Forces' victory has pushed the Communists back to a new continuous line running through Kaesong and Hwachon.

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance04_M
entries.text: Following their rapid withdrawal and our swift advance, Red Forces have occupied new positions on a continuous front running from Sariwon to Pyonggang.

entries.name: IDS_T_Advance05_M
entries.text: The Communists have ceased their northward flight and are preparing to face UN Forces advancing towards their Capital.

entries.name: IDS_T_Retreat01_M
entries.text: Country-wide, UN Forces have dropped back to a new line. Chinese forces are pursuing as quickly as they can.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest05_M
entries.text: The Reds are attacking to the north-west of Pyongsan.

entries.name: IDS_D_BritsArrive_M
entries.text: British troops will be arriving in South Korea today to participate in the UN defense.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPDESC0
entries.text:
On the morning of 25  June 1950, North Korean troops spearheaded by T-34 tanks swept south of the 38th parallel. Unprepared, ROK Forces were driven back. In the month that followed, US aircraft pounded advancing communist columns. These aerial strikes were so punishing that the North Koreans were compelled to restrict their movements to the hours of darkness.
In the Air, American Jets outclassed the North Korean Airforce and effectively removed it as a threat to the allied ground forces.
Troops were hastily committed to the effort. Elements of the 24th US Infantry Division were moved into place, south of Seoul, to blunt an enemy thrust down the western corridor. Outnumbered 10 to 1, the Americans could only hope to fight a delaying action until reinforcements arrived.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPDESC1
entries.text:
By the beginning of August, UN Forces were contracting to form a perimeter around the port of Pusan where vital supplies and reinforcements were landed continuously. The attacking North Koreans grew weaker as their over-stretched supply lines were increasingly compromised by interdiction sorties.
Sensing that the last chance for victory was slipping away, the North Koreans launched many bloody and desperate assaults along the perimeter. Although some gains were made, superior UN firepower stopped the Communists from making a significant breakthrough.
Unlike their adversaries, UN troops could call on aircraft for assistance. The F51 Mustang, capable of operating from dirt strips, flew constant sorties along the front in support of UN troops.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPDESC2
entries.text:
On September 18, following the marine landing at Inchon, the 8th Army commenced its breakout from the Pusan pocket. Faced with capture or destruction, the communist forces had no option but to flee northwards in disarray. Snapping at their heels, UN troops rapidly closed the net across the south of the country.
On September 27, UN Forces crossed the 38th parallel with orders to achieve the total destruction of the North Korean army. From the air, UN aircraft inflicted telling blows on the retreating enemy.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPDESC3
entries.text:
During November, UN Forces engaged in combat with infiltrating Chinese forces as they advanced to the Yalu River. UN aircraft tasked with cutting links to China came under increasing pressure from Chinese MiG 15s. The F80 was outclassed. The USAF responded to this threat by dispatching a wing each of F86A Sabres and F84E Thunderjets to Korea.
On November 20, the Chinese launched a massive offensive that sent UN forces reeling southwards. Large numbers of UN troops, cut-off by the rapid enemy advance, escaped to the eastern ports under cover of airforce fire.
Bridges and supply routes were hammered by UN bombers to stem the flow of Chinese Forces. The Sabres and Thunderjets flew CAP and escort missions along the Yalu to protect the vulnerable bombers.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPDESC4
entries.text:
The UN continued its withdrawal throughout the early days of '51 to successive lines below the 38th parallel. An attempt to hold Seoul failed and UN troops were evacuated from the docks at Inchon. The situation was a complete reversal of events from the previous year.
The Chinese advance began to stall. Their over-stretched supply lines could not cope with demands from the front. The UN eventually stabilized a new front between Pyongtaek and Wonju from which they were able to launch new operations aimed at regaining the initiative.
Communist Forces recoiled and waited for a new and bigger supply base to form in the forward areas. With the supplies in place they would be able to launch their Spring Offensive.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPOBJ0
entries.text: Inflict heavy casualties on the advancing enemy columns. The enemy push must be slowed or the 24th Division's blocking positions will be overrun.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPOBJ1
entries.text: Fly ground attack missions against NKA Forces attacking in the Taegu sector. The perimeter must hold. An enemy breakthrough will fold the allied defense.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPOBJ2
entries.text: You must destroy supply dumps and bridges that feed supplies to the rapidly crumbling North Korean Army. Any NKA forces that fight their way out of the UN net will rally in the north.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPOBJ3
entries.text: Ensure that your bombers can operate safely in the skies of North Korea by flying close escort missions and barrier patrols along the Yalu. The bombers must hit targets that will slow the flow of Chinese men and materials into the Northwest.

entries.name: IDS_CAMPOBJ4
entries.text:
The UN must hold out until the end of Spring, at which point the Reds will abandon their offensive and agree to talk peace. During this period, you must prevent supplies from reaching front-line positions by hitting supply dumps and bridges, and also assist friendly ground forces directly by flying close air support missions.
The war is lost if UN Forces are pushed from their starting positions at Pyongtaek and Wonju. If the UN takes the North's Capital, Pyongyang, then the war is concluded with a UN victory.

entries.name: IDS_ALPHAREQUIRED
entries.text: Insufficient aircraft for required Alpha Strike.

entries.name: IDS_T_Supply00_M
entries.text: Supplies are being stockpiled at this location. We recommend a strike as soon as possible.

entries.name: IDS_T_Supply01_M
entries.text: Interdiction sorties are successfully limiting the number of supplies that replenish this front. Poorly armed and under-nourished enemy soldiers have been surrendering to our forward patrols.

entries.name: IDS_T_Supply02_M
entries.text: We believe that the enemy have more than adequate supplies in this area. They seem capable of matching our Force's ammunition expenditure during artillery exchanges. We must step up our interdiction efforts.

entries.name: IDS_T_FAC00_M
entries.text: FACs on this front report that close air support missions have severely punished enemy ground forces.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army00_M
entries.text: Activity along this front indicates that the Reds may be preparing to launch a new assault.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army01_M
entries.text: The situation on this front has become untenable and our Forces are attempting to pull back before they are trapped.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army02_M
entries.text: Our forward units report that Chinese Forces have ceased attacking and are moving back to their line of departure. The Sector Commander is confident that the attack has been repulsed and the line can be stabilized.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army03_M
entries.text: Chinese troops have suddenly broken contact and are racing back to their prepared positions. The Local Commander believes that their supplies were insufficient to sustain the assault.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army04_H
entries.text: All UN Corps withdraw to new line

entries.name: IDS_T_Army04_M
entries.text: The collapse of UN resistance on this front has resulted in all friendly forces abandoning this line and falling back to prepared reserve positions.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army05_M
entries.text: A UN offensive in this sector is being planned. A major interdiction effort against the MSR will create the ideal conditions for an assault on this front.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army06_M
entries.text: Chinese Forces in this sector have been defeated. All Red Forces along this line are making a tactical withdrawal to a new reserve line. UN Ground Forces are rapidly consolidating their gains.

entries.name: IDS_T_Army07_M
entries.text: The Local Commander reports unacceptably high UN losses in this sector. The order to disengage from combat and abort the current offensive has been issued.

entries.name: IDS_T_FAC01_M
entries.text: Mosquito pilots report fierce combat in this sector. They claim that without immediate and substantial air support, UN ground units will suffer severe losses.

entries.name: IDS_T_FAC02_M
entries.text: UN troops claim that they are suffering unnecessary casualties due to pilots making inaccurate strikes against targets close to their positions.

entries.name: IDS_T_FAC03_M
entries.text: Our Forces are making good gains on this front. The enemy's main line of defense has been broken.

entries.name: IDS_D_Flak00_M
entries.text: Intelligence division has noted a serious increase in the number of anti-aircraft defenses along the main rail-lines routes.

entries.name: IDS_D_Flak01_M
entries.text: The volume of anti-aircraft fire encountered at enemy supply points has increased significantly.

entries.name: IDS_D_Flak02_M
entries.text: AAA coverage along main roads and highways has reached a new and disturbing level.

entries.name: IDS_D_Flak03_M
entries.text: The Reds have increased flak coverage at all major bridges and choke points.

entries.name: IDS_T_Bombers00_M
entries.text: Our medium bomber losses are unacceptable. Until further notice, all B29 squadrons are standing down from duty.

entries.name: IDS_T_Bombers01_M
entries.text: Our B29s are available for duty again. Ensure that you protect them adequately or you will lose their services again.

entries.name: IDS_T_FAC04_M
entries.text: Communist forces have infiltrated to the rear to set up roadblocks. The situation has become critical. UN Forces remaining at the front risk being cut off from supplies and support.

entries.name: IDS_T_Retreat02_M
entries.text: Our defeat in a key sector has thrown the entire allied line back to old positions.

entries.name: IDS_T_Retreat03_M
entries.text: The Reds have pushed our entire defensive line southwards to new positions.

entries.name: IDS_D_Repairs00_M
entries.text: Repair materials are being stored nearby so repairs can be undertaken immediately.

entries.name: IDS_D_RailInterdict00_M
entries.text: Our interdiction efforts against the communist rail net are proving effective. Recent reconn shows that there is little change in the number of boxcars at principal marshalling yards, signifying that they are unable to move out.

entries.name: IDS_T_AF00_M
entries.text: Enemy forces are dangerously close to this airfield. Staff and crews are prepared to evacuate key equipment and aircraft the instant you issue the order.

entries.name: IDS_T_AF01_M
entries.text: The enemy now controls this airfield.

entries.name: IDS_T_AF02_M
entries.text: This airfield is now in UN hands. Our maintenance crews are moving in to ready the field for use.

entries.name: IDS_D_Repairs01_M
entries.text: The enemy makes good use of its huge civilian labor force. It takes the same amount of time to fill one hundred craters as it does to fill one.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield00_M
entries.text: MiGs have appeared over the Yalu in great numbers.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield01_M
entries.text: Enemy Squadrons have taken up residence on the airfield. The field is fully operational.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield02_M
entries.text: This airfield has been rapidly evacuated in response to the increasingly close proximity of enemy Ground Forces. Staff are unhappy that some equipment has been left behind.

entries.name: IDS_T_DefendWest06_M
entries.text: The enemy are making a major effort to push UN troops away from their Capital.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield04_M
entries.text: Enemy squadrons have pulled out of this airfield.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield05_M
entries.text: This airfield has been abandoned. The enemy does not have enough resources to maintain the base while under constant pressure from air strikes.

entries.name: IDS_T_Airfield06_M
entries.text: We have discovered that this airfield is actually non-operational. The field has been set up with dummy aircraft that appear real from a distance. The enemy obviously intend for us to expend resources hitting a target of little military value.

entries.name: IDS_T_USE_DAMAGED_WH_M
entries.text: Reconn shows that the enemy are using damaged warehousing for storage.

entries.name: IDS_T_Replenish_M
entries.text: A new batch of aircraft have arrived from the States. Your most depleted squadron will receive up to four new aircraft.

entries.name: IDS_D_AlJolson_M
entries.text: Al Jolson has arrived in Korea to entertain the troops. Order your tickets now.

entries.name: IDS_T_SquadRotate_M
entries.text: We have noted the arrival of a new MiG squadron. We believe that an existing squadron has been rotated out en masse, much to our advantage.

entries.name: IDS_T_StoodDown_M
entries.text: We have noted there is an entire MiG squadron that has little desire to face our fighters. On the occasions when they enter MiG Alley they will not descend to engage.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText00_M
entries.text:
The NKAF is posing a serious threat. On June 27, 2 Yaks strafed Kimpo airfield, hitting the tower, a fuel dump and a C54 transport plane.

MISSION BRIEF:
You are ordered to perform an armed-reconn mission along the main rail-line leading into Seoul from Munsan-ni.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText01_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Follow the northern road from Wonju to Hoengsong and engage any traffic.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText02_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Patrol the area between Suwon and Osan and engage North Korean support columns.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText03_M
entries.text: Patrol the highway between Osan and Pyongtaek. Enemy columns are pushing rapidly through this area.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText06_M
entries.text:
At least five NKA divisions are reportedly massing near Wagewan, 20 miles north west of Taegu. They may have tank support.

MISSION BRIEF:
Hit the enemy units before they deploy against our front line positions.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText07_M
entries.text:
The enemy assault is now underway in the Taegu sector. NKA forces are attempting to drive a wedge between the ROK 1st and 6th Divisions.

MISSION BRIEF:
Fly close air support along the Taegu front.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText08_M
entries.text:
A regiment of the US 24th Division is being dispatched to this sector to bolster the defense.

MISSION BRIEF:
Fly Close Air Support.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText09_M
entries.text:
Elements of the British 27th brigade have arrived in Korea. Battalion from the Middlesex Regiment and the Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders are being deployed north of Taegu.

MISSION BRIEF:
Fly Close Air Support.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText12_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
You are tasked with destroying a Supply Facility.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText13_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Your target is a key river crossing.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText14_M
entries.text:
Premier Chou En-Lai has warned the United Nations that China will come to the aid of North Korea if the latter's territory is invaded.

MISSION BRIEF:
Destroy a Supply Dump

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText15_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Attack a warehouse facility.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText16_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Attack a warehouse facility.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText18_M
entries.text:
We believe Sinuiju to be the seat of Kim Il Sung's Fugitive Government.

MISSION BRIEF:
Fly cover for a B29 raid on this important city.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText19_M
entries.text:
Increasing numbers of Chinese 'volunteers' are entering North Korea. We have authorization to attack the bridges linking Sinuiju with Antung in China.

MISSION BRIEF:
Patrol the Yalu and prevent Chinese MiGs from interfering with our B-29 attack against the Yalu bridges.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText20_M
entries.text:
It appears that our intelligence greatly underestimated the number of Chinese Troops already present in Korea.

MISSION BRIEF:
Perform a MiGCAP along the Yalu River.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText21_M
entries.text:
Chinese Forces have launched shock attacks against our troops. The entire 8th army is reeling backwards under the weight of superior numbers.

MISSION BRIEF:
Patrol the area opposite Antung and hold back any intruding MiGs. Our bombers are concentrating against targets all along the Yalu.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText22_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Fly a barrier patrol along the Yalu to the Northeast of Sinuiju.

entries.name: IDS_D_MiniText23_M
entries.text:
MISSION BRIEF:
Maintain air superiority over Sinuiju while our medium bombers make another attempt against the Yalu bridges.

entries.name: IDS_ADDTOVISITORSBOOK
entries.text: Add my name to the Host's Visitors Book

entries.name: IDS_REINSTALLORPREFS
entries.text:
Please reinstall the drivers for this joystick
or select a different joystick from Preferences
before entering the 3d.

entries.name: IDS_RECONNECTORPREFS
entries.text:
Please reconnect this joystick
or select a different joystick from Preferences
before entering the 3d.

entries.name: IDS_CONFIGIGNORED
entries.text: Your configuration file is out of date and has been ignored.

entries.name: IDS_CHOOSEPREFS
entries.text: Please check your preferences and joystick settings.

entries.name: IDS_EDITSLOST
entries.text: Edits to your missions will be lost.

entries.name: IDS_SETMISSIONS
entries.text: An easy way to set up missions is to go to the Directives.

entries.name: IDS_NOTINTERESTING
entries.text: This area is not thought to have any strategic importance.

entries.name: IDS_NOTSLOTS
entries.text: One or more players have not chosen a slot

entries.name: IDS_NOTSESSION
entries.text: Could not create session or player

entries.name: IDS_LOG_UNSEEN
entries.text: A group of %i %s were reported by intelligence operatives near %s, but we didn't see them.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP1ENDGOOD
entries.text: Telling blows from UN air strikes have compelled the NKPA to restrict its movements to the hours of darkness. Along with the delaying action fought by the 24th Division, enough time has been bought for reinforcements landing at Pusan to be deployed along the Naktong River.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP2ENDGOOD
entries.text: Aided by excellent air support, UN troops have held back repeated attacks from superior numbers of NKPA troops. This has bought enough time for MacArthur to launch his bold counter-stroke: an amphibious landing at Inchon that will open up a second front far behind Communist Lines.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP3ENDGOOD
entries.text: UN air power has inflicted serious damage along the enemy's lines of retreat. Large numbers of NKPA troops have been captured or destroyed. UN and ROK troops have encountered light resistance as they envelop the North's Capital, Pyongyang.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP4ENDGOOD
entries.text: The Chinese attempt to cut off the 8th army has failed. Although some heavy equipment was abandoned, most troop units managed to escape intact. X Corps was successfully evacuated from the port of Hungnam along with its gear and 80,000 refugees. Our bombers and fighters have played a key role in holding up the enemy advance.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP1ENDBAD
entries.text: UN air power failed to stem the North Korean advance and as a result the 24th Division was rolled up. NKPA forces moved rapidly on the port of Pusan before UN reinforcements could be deployed, leaving UN commanders little choice but to order a mass evacuation. The whole of Korea is now in Communist hands.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP2ENDBAD
entries.text: NKPA forces have broken through the perimeter and the entire front has folded. UN forces are pulling back to Pusan where a mass evacuation is underway. Korea will be abandoned to the Communist aggressors.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP3ENDBAD
entries.text: The UN failed to capitalize on the NKPA's retreat. Enough of the Red Army escaped northwards to constitute a serious and continued threat.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP4ENDBAD
entries.text: MiG Alley has proved a no-go area for allied bombers. Even America's premier fighter was not equal to the task of protecting the bombers from Chinese MiG 15s. As a result, vast numbers of Chinese troops have swept across the north of Korea to trap whole UN Divisions.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END00
entries.text: UN Forces have captured Pyongyang and Chinese forces are retreating northwards. Delegations have arrived at Panmunjom to discuss peace. It is believed that the high cost of sustaining the war and continued pressure from UN Forces will cause the Chinese to abandon North Korea.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END01
entries.text: Delegations have finally gathered around the peace-table at Panmunjom to talk terms. The UN is in a favorable position. The front has been pushed north of the 38th parallel and the Communist delegation seems eager to conclude talks quickly in order to freeze the front before any more of the North is lost to them.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END02
entries.text: Delegations have finally gathered around the peace-table at Panmunjom to talk terms. The UN is in a favorable position. The front has been pushed north of the 38th parallel and the Communist delegation seems eager to conclude talks quickly in order to freeze the front before any more of the North is lost to them.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END03
entries.text: Peace-talks have been initiated. Delegates have arrived at Panmunjom to talk terms. The Communists have built deep bunkers and interlocking positions along a line near to the 38th parallel. Fighting will continue in this area for another two years while the delegates at the Peace Table argue over terms.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END04
entries.text: Peace-talks have been initiated. Delegates have arrived at Panmunjom to talk terms. The Communists have built deep bunkers and interlocking positions along a line near to the 38th parallel. Fighting will continue in this area for another 2 years while the delegates at the Peace Table argue over terms.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END05
entries.text: Delegations from both sides have arrived at Panmunjom to discuss peace. The front line is situated below the 38th parallel and this puts the UN in a poor position. Talks are proving difficult, with the Communist delegation believing it is in a position to dictate terms and ROK president Syngman Rhee demanding that the talks be abandoned in favor of continued military action.

entries.name: IDS_CAMP5_END06
entries.text: Morale is at an all-time low. This is the third time that the UN alliance has been driven back into the tip of the peninsula. Commanders agree that unless drastic action is taken, by increasing ground strength and expanding the air campaign into Mainland China, the South will ultimately be lost. The War is escalating...

entries.name: IDS_LOG_SEENBYOTHERS
entries.text: %i %s out of %s were encountered by another air group over %s.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_SEENBYPLAYERS
entries.text: We saw a squadron of about %i %s from %s over %s. It was not engaged.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_HOSTILE
entries.text: A hostile squadron of %i %s from %s was encountered by our group near %s. We did not engage.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_SPOTTEDDOGFIGHT
entries.text: A dogfight involving over %i %s was spotted near %s. We left them to it.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_APPROACHED
entries.text: Spottted %i %s from %s near %s. We got close enough to make an I.D but not close enough for combat.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_ATTACKED
entries.text: Identified and attacked a group of %i %s operating out of %s. Hope we have more luck next time we patrol near %s.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_DAMAGED
entries.text: We mixed it up with a group of %i %s from %s while patrolling near %s. We definitely landed some hits!

entries.name: IDS_LOG_KILLED
entries.text: Engaged a group of %i %s from %s and definitely bagged some kills. Our designated patrol region was %s.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_TAKEOFF
entries.text: Our flight of %i %s took off from %s.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_BENTWING
entries.text: Bent a wing on my %s. Any landing you walk away from...

entries.name: IDS_LOG_FATALCRASH
entries.text: I often watched my buddy writing in this log. Now he is gone I feel I should add my own words about how he met his end...

entries.name: IDS_LOG_CRASHLAND
entries.text: Had to bring my %s down before reaching %s. Is it too far to walk from %s?

entries.name: IDS_LOG_CRASHLAND2FAST
entries.text: The %s was too damaged to fly back to %s. I put it down at %s and was lucky to walk away from the wreckage.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_LANDOK
entries.text: I had to bring my %s down before getting to %s. No real problems with the landing, much to the relief of the ground teams at %s.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_MIDAIRCOLLISION
entries.text: A mid-air collision finished today's flying.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_DAMAGEDSTATIC
entries.text: Got some good hits on the target. Not enough to take it out without another pass.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_DAMAGEDTRUCK
entries.text: Raked %i vehicles near %s. Didn't see any explosions, though.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_KILLEDSTATIC
entries.text: I can report that the Primary Target was successfully destroyed.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_KILLEDTRUCK
entries.text: Engaged a %i strong convoy near %s. We went in hard and got a number of hits on target.

entries.name: IDS_LOG_PARACHUTED
entries.text: Really lucky to parachute out of the mess.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXYalu0
entries.text: The Twin Yalu bridges link the gateway city of Sinuiju with Antung in China. The road and combination road/rail bridges are heavily defended. During winter the Yalu River often freezes to the point where heavy goods can be carried across the ice.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXSinuiju0
entries.text: One road and one rail bridge (close and almost parallel) cross a branch of the Yalu River south of Sinuiju en route to Yomju. The bridges have some wooden components.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXUngig
entries.text: This road bridge lies in a valley between Kanggye and Pungsan. It is mainly constructed from timber.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXchosan
entries.text: A single road bridge crosses the Yalu further inland where the river is narrower. This solid construction carries the road to Huichon.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXTaeryong
entries.text: A large double track rail bridge and one small road bridge cross the Taeryong River north of Sinanju.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXSinanju0
entries.text: Two steel-trestle rail bridges cross the river Chongchon to the north side of Sinanju. A highway bridge crosses further up river to the east.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXKilchu
entries.text: This is a reinforced highway bridge in the rolling hills just north of Kimchaek.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXPuktaechon
entries.text: One road and one rail bridge cross a fork in the river just south of Tanchon on the east coast. The less sturdy road bridge is at the end of the northern fork.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXNamdaechon
entries.text: This single track rail bridge spans a large valley near Pukchong. The bridge has six spans and three concrete supports.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXKumjinchon
entries.text: This solid rail bridge crosses the Kumjinchon River on the East Coast.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXManpo
entries.text: This wooden rail bridge is constructed in the difficult terrain south of Manpo.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXHuichon
entries.text: This reinforced rail bridge is in the hills south of Kangye.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXNan
entries.text: The Nan-gang River is crossed by a single wooden rail bridge in the hills east of the northern capital.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXPyongyang
entries.text: This concrete and steel rail bridge sits in the heart of Pyongyang City. It consists of eighteen steel spans.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXInje
entries.text: This road bridge is situated in a fairly mountainous wooded area near Chuncheon.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXPyongyang0
entries.text: Two solidly engineered road bridges lie in the center of Pyongyang city.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXYesong
entries.text: This large double track rail bridge crosses the Han-gang River just to the north west of Munsan in a slightly hilly area. The bridge has fifteen steel spans and fourteen concrete support struts.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXTokohig
entries.text: A small wooden rail bridge spans the river just south of Yonhung. This bridge has only four spans and two supports.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXKowan
entries.text: A small wooden single track rail bridge crosses a river to the south of Yonhung on the East Coast.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXImjin
entries.text: A small wooden single track rail bridge spans the Imjin River in quite a hilly area.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXHan
entries.text: A large concrete road bridge spans the Han-gang River on Route 13, five miles east of Seoul. Due to its construction of eleven supports and fifteen spans, this bridge is capable of carrying heavy vehicles.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXPukhan0
entries.text: The single track rail line crosses the Han-gang at a fork where it becomes the Bughan-gang River.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXKapyong
entries.text: Two bridges cross the Bughan-gang River at separate points. A bridge at Kapyong carries the double track rail-line and the road crossing is further upriver near Chuncheon. Expanses of forest cover the area.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXChonui
entries.text: This small wooden rail bridge is situated in a slightly hilly area just west of Chonui. This bridge has two support struts and five spans.

entries.name: IDS_BrXXSeoul
entries.text:
Four bridges cross the Han-gang River to the South-east of Seoul; three steel cantilever bridges serve the main rail-line and a concrete highway bridge slightly further to the east handles heavy traffic.

Historical Note:
One bridge earned the title 'Elastic Bridge' due to its refusal to fall.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_WhRdSinuiju
entries.text: Sinuiju is the most important gateway into North Korea. It is heavily defended with coverage from AAA on the Chinese bank of the Yalu. MiGs flying from four main bases just over the river can enter Korean air space in a matter of minutes.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdSonchon
entries.text: Sonchon is a supply-center connected by rail to Sinanju, north of the Chongchon River.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SCCSakchu
entries.text: Sakchu is situated near the Yalu River and it is home to the North Korean Military Academy.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SCCChosan
entries.text:
Chosan is a supply gateway located near the Yalu River.

Historical Note:
ROK troops arrived here on 26 Oct.1950 - shortly before the Chinese invaded.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_MyRlChongjin
entries.text: Chongjin has two major harbors, a railway yard and workshops. The shattered ruins of the Mitsubishi Iron company are here.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_MyRdHamhung
entries.text:
Hamhung is an important port on the East Coast. It sends supplies throughout the region. The docks are an important military asset.

Historical Note:
UN troops were evacuated from the docks during the Chinese advance in December 1950.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SCCManpo
entries.text: Manpo is one of three gateway cities into North Korea. Supplies are brought here then filtered out to the supply dispersal points.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SCCKangye
entries.text: Home to a large arsenal and communication center.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SCCHuichon
entries.text: This is a supply dispersal point.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_WhRdSukchon
entries.text:
Historical Note:
Following the UN advance into Pyongyang on 19 Oct. 51, the 187th Airborne RCT parachuted into a drop zone at Sukchon with the aim of cutting off retreating NKPA troops.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_MyRdPyongyang
entries.text: The Capital and Arsenal of North Korea has armament plants, large freight yards and major railway plants.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_ICMPNampo
entries.text:
This port has anchorage for ships of any draft.

Historical Note:
In the opening stages of hostilities UN medium bombers destroyed the various metal production plants located here.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdSariwon
entries.text: Sariwon has a supply depot connected to Pyongyang by rail track.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdKaesong
entries.text:
Historical Note:
Kaesong was the site of the first cease-fire conferences from 10 July to 2 August 1951. They later resumed at nearby Panmunjom following charges and counter-charges of neutrality violations.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_MyRlWonsan
entries.text:
Wonsan is a major seaport and one of the three most important rail-hubs in Korea.

Historical Note: It was the main site for petroleum refining in North Korea before the start of hostilities.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdKumhwa
entries.text:
Kumhwa has a vital supply depot and also holds one of three major Communist Communications Centers.

Historical Note:
Kumhwa, along with Pyonggang and Chorwon, formed an area that came to be known as 'The Iron Triangle'.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdYangdok
entries.text:
This town sits on a vital lateral supply link that runs from the west coast to the east.

Historical Note:
This area was so well defended that it came to be known as 'Death Valley' by pilots.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdPyonggang
entries.text:
Historical Note:
Pyonggang was the Northern most point of an area that was named 'The Iron Triangle' due to a good supply network to the rear and good defensive positions to the front. Kumhwa and Chorwon form the other two points that mark the region.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_MyBlSeoul
entries.text:
When communist forces operate south of the 38th parallel, the focal point of their distribution system is Seoul. It is believed that around two thirds of all supplies pass through here.

Historical Note:
The South's capital changed hands four times during the course of the war. By the spring of 1951 much of the city had been reduced to rubble.

entries.name: IDS_TARG_SdRdWonju
entries.text:
Wonju is a key road-junction city nestled in a mountain basin of central Korea.

Historical Note:
The Wonju-Chipyongni area was denied to the enemy by the heroic stand of UN Forces in Jan. - Feb. 1951.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSinmak
entries.text:
Runway: 5500 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

Sinmak is the first airfield north of the 38th Parallel, supported by Sariwon and Anak.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXHwangju
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities: Poor

Hwangju is a small airfield located below Pyongyang.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSinuiju
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Earth surface
Support: 3 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Good

Located in the North West of Korea, just south of the Yalu river.

Historical Note:
Sinuiju and Uiju were the only airfields in Korea to receive MiGs, the rest being kept out of action.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSunan
entries.text:
Runway: 6000 ft. Hard surface
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Sunan is one of the larger Communist airfields and is well defended by AAA sites.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXAntung
entries.text:
Runway: 7200 ft. Concrete surface
Support: 3 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Excellent

The Antung Airfield Complex is one of the largest and most modern in China. It has a radar system used to vector its fighters. On a good day UN pilots patrolling the Yalu can see MiGs leaving Antung.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXTaTung
entries.text:
Runway: 6800 ft. Hard surface
Support: 3 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Good

This major Chinese airfield is not as advanced as its sister field, Antung. Few of the aircraft revetments are used. The Communists are confident that they are protected from UN air attack.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXUiju
entries.text:
Runway: 7000 ft. Hard surface
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Uiju, most northern of the Korean airfields, is well defended by radar-controlled flak and by more than fifty searchlights.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXAnak
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities: Poor

Anak has a short airstrip with a poorly maintained surface. Its limited facilities contain some aircraft revetments.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSariwon
entries.text:
Runway: 4200 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

Located just above the 38th parallel. Only necessary repairs are made in order to keep the airfield serviceable.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXYongyu
entries.text:
Runway: 6000 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

Yongyu is located on the North West Coast of Korea, south of Sinanju

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSinanju
entries.text:
Runway: 6200 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPyongni
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

This short earth airstrip is under the protective umbrella of the three jet airfields - Namsi, Taechon and Saamcham.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXTaechon
entries.text:
Runway: 5500 ft. Hard surface
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Taechon is one of three important North Korean jet airfields, including Saamcham and Namsi.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXNamsi
entries.text:
Runway: 5900 ft. Hard surface
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Namsi, like Taechon and Saamcham, is one of three jet airfields. The airfield plays an important role along side Taechon and Saamcham in the Chongchon area.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSaamcham
entries.text:
Runway: 5700 ft. Hard surface
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Similar in size and facilities to its sister airfields, Namsi and Taechon, Saamcham plays an important role in the Chongchon area.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPyongyangE
entries.text:
Runway: 4000 ft. Hard surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities:    Average

Located north-east of Pyongyang City. The airstrip has reasonable facilities with protective revetments for the aircraft.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPyongyangM
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities:     Excellent

Pyongyang Main is the biggest of the three airfields at Pyongyang. The North Korean Air Force Headquarters are located nearby in the city.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPyongyangD
entries.text:
Runway: 5760 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

This is an ingenious use of resources. A downtown street has been demolished to create a concrete runway. Support facilities are housed in the residential area with side streets acting as taxi-ways.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXKimpo
entries.text:
Runway: 6400 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 3 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Excellent

The South's International Airport is the largest and most modern in Korea.

Historical Note:
During the course of the war it changed hands many times. When it wasn't being demolished, it was being expanded by UN engineers.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSeoul
entries.text:
Runway: 4000 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

This small earth airstrip is generally neglected when the facilities at nearby Kimpo are available.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXChuncheon
entries.text:
Runway: 4000 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

Chuncheon has a small clay and gravel runway. The airfield is primarily used to aid supply drops for front line troops.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXOsan
entries.text:
Runway: 9000 ft. Hard surface
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities: Excellent

Historical Note:
This airfield was greatly upgraded by UN engineers over a period of years

entries.name: IDS_AfXXSuwon
entries.text:
Runway: 5000 ft. Concrete
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Historical Note:
Suwon was practically rebuilt by UN engineers over the course of three years.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPyongtaek
entries.text:
Runway: 5500 ft. Earth strip with PSP
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

entries.name: IDS_AfXXKunsan
entries.text:
Runway: 4800 ft. Hard surface
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities: Average

This small airfield is found on the West Coast of Southern Korea near the port of Kunsan.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXTaejon
entries.text:
Runway: 5200 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

Due to the dirt surface and poor drainage the airfield is only capable of supporting larger transports when the ground is frozen.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXTaegu
entries.text:
Runway: 5300 ft. Clay and Gravel with PSP
Support: 2 Jet squadrons
Facilities: Average

Taegu's clay and gravel based runway assumes a new importance when enemy forces push below the 38th parallel. It is the rear-most jet capable field in Korea. Beyond that, jets must fly from Japan.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXTaeguW
entries.text:
Runway: 3500 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Poor

entries.name: IDS_AfXXKangnung
entries.text:
Runway: 4600 ft. Dirt surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

Kangnung is located on the East Coast of Korea.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXPohang
entries.text:
Runway: 5000 ft. PSP
Support: 1 Jet squadron
Facilities: Average

Located on the south east coast near the fishing village of Pohang. The surrounding area has been drained to allow for taxiways and other extra facilities.

entries.name: IDS_AfXXKangdong
entries.text:
Runway: 6000 ft. Hard surface
Support: 1 Prop squadron
Facilities: Average

entries.name: IDS_BLUEINITIATIVE
entries.text: The UN Forces have the initiative.

entries.name: IDS_CT_DESIGNTEAMTITLE
entries.text:
Game Design:

Rod Hyde, Mark Shaw

entries.name: IDS_CT_ARTWORKTEAMTITLE
entries.text:
Artwork Team:

Andy McCann, Richard Jones, Toks Solarin

entries.name: IDS_CT_EITEAMTITLE

entries.name: IDS_CT_TESTINGTEAMTITLE
entries.text:
Testing Team:

Carlo Bush, Ian Hardy, Ben Jay, Chris Jones, Tomek Ochocki, Danny Rawles, Iain Riches

entries.name: IDS_INSERTCD
entries.text: Please insert the MiG Alley CD.

entries.name: IDS_FILENOTEXIST
entries.text: File does not exist for reading(%x=%s).

entries.name: IDS_PLAYERDIED
entries.text:
Your Commander's career has ended. He has been lost in action.


Click on QUIT to start a new campaign.

Click on CONTINUE to continue the campaign under the leadership of a new Commander.

Click on LOAD GAME to get back to the beginning of the last session.

entries.name: IDS_NOTCLEARED
entries.text: You are not cleared to fly Bombers.

entries.name: IDS_RESWARNING
entries.text: MiG Alley requires a minimum Desktop setting of High Color at 800x600. For best results use True Color at 1280x1024.
